text: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have been widely adopted as one of the primary ways to model graph-structured data in recommender systems. However, in practice, the underlying recommendation environment is often highly complex and heterogeneous, which contains a variety of social and knowledge-based relationships for users and items, which can provide abundant semantic information for improving user preference learning. Therefore, it is imperative to study the heterogeneous graph-enhanced relational learning. While contrastive self-supervised learning has drawn significant attention in the research community, current solutions for modeling multi-relational graphs are not optimal. To address this issue, we propose a novel framework named Heterogeneous Graph Contrastive Learning (HGCL) that enhances user-item interaction learning by contrastively transferring relational knowledge from multiple graph views for better fusing heterogeneous relational semantics. Moreover, the side information in heterogeneous graphs is often subjective, so we deploy a set of meta-networks to serve as a personalized knowledge transformer to augment the contrastive augmentation process in a learning-to-augmento (LatM) fashion, which adaptively transforms the heterogeneous graphs into optimal contrastive learning objects for each user and item pair. Experiments on three large-scale datasets validate the superiority of HGCL over the state-of-the-art recommendation methods, and ablation studies confirm the contributions of each component. Our implementation code is available at https://github.com/HKUDS/HGCL.
